title: Sorbus concolor
taxonomy: Sorbus concolor is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Sorbus, which includes several other species of rowan and whitebeam trees. The family Rosaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes roses, apples, pears, and many other fruit trees and shrubs. Within the genus Sorbus, Sorbus concolor is classified as a member of the subgenus Sorbus. The classification of Sorbus concolor reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the family Rosaceae.

Its berries, bark, and leaves have been utilized to create remedies for fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. In traditional Chinese medicine, Sorbus concolor is believed to have a cooling effect and is used to balance the body's energy. intro: Sorbus concolor, also known as the white rowan or mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountains of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Sorbus concolor include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Sorbus concolor is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in the production of extracts and supplements.
